chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C.P No.D-2206 of 2025 Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on office objections. 2. For orders on CMA No.8751/2025 (Ex/A). 3. For hearing of main case. 24.02.2026 Mr. Abdul Naeem Pirzada, Advocate for petitioner..-.-.-. -.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- The petitioner has approached this Court seeking appointment under the deceased Quota in Government Service under the erstwhile Rule 11-A of the Sindh Civil Servants (Appointment, Promotion and Transfer) Rules, 1974. It is pertinent to note that the Honorable Supreme Court of Pakistan, in the case of General Post Office, Islamabad v. Muhammad Jalal (PLD 2024 Supreme Court 1276), has declared such provisions to be ultra vires the Constitution. In view of the above authoritative pronouncement, learned counsel for the petitioner has been unable to distinguish the present case or to point out any lawful basis to grant the relief sought. Consequently, no writ can be issued for facilitating employment on the grounds claimed by the petitioner. According, the instant petition is dismissed in limine. JUDGE JUDGE S.Nawaz(St)’
